data.title: OneWater Marine reports mixed Q4 results; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• OneWater Marine press release (ONEW): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 misses by $0.21.

  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share1 for fiscal fourth quarter 2025 was less than $0.01, compared to adjusted diluted loss per share1 of $(0.36) in 2024.

    Fiscal fourth quarter 2025 Adjusted EBITDA1 increased 123.0% to $17.5 million compared to $7.8 million for fiscal fourth quarter 2024.

  • Revenue of $460.14M (+21.8% Y/Y) beats by $52.56M.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance

    For fiscal full-year 2026, OneWater anticipates industry-wide unit sales to be flat year over year. When factoring in the lost revenue from exited brands, the Company expects dealership same-store sales to be flat year over year and total revenue to be in the range of $1.83 billion to $1.93 billion vs. $1.88B consensus. Adjusted EBITDA2 is expected to be in the range of $65 million to $85 million and adjusted diluted earnings per share2 is expected to be in the range of $0.25 to $0.75 vs. $1.23 consensus.

data.title: Retail hiring slows ahead of holiday season; Target rolls out new smile mandate
data.description:
Retail hiring in October fell below pre-pandemic levels for seasonal hiring, indicating weaker expectations for consumer spending this holiday season.

Retail-related job postings - including retail shops, hospitality and food services - dropped 16% in October from a year ago, Reuters reported, citing data from the Indeed Hiring Lab.

Retailers may limit hiring for the holiday season due to the added burden of tariffs, weakening consumer sentiment and the government shutdown, said Indeed Hiring Lab senior economist Daniel Culbertson.

The National Retail Federation expects retailers to hire 265,000-365,000 seasonal workers this year, compared to 442,000 seasonal hires in 2024.

"What we've seen is a lot fewer retailers go public and state that they're going to hire 'X' amount of people," said Mark Mathews, NRF chief economist, adding that companies may hire more later in the season.

Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Macy's (M) and UPS (UPS) have not announced their holiday hiring plans as they had done in previous years. Kroger (KR) is hiring over 18,000 seasonal workers this year, down from 25,000 last year.

But Amazon (AMZN) is hiring 250,000 people this holiday season, the same level as the last two years.

"It's possible with rate cuts and a strong showing in November, companies may make a late season push for employees, but at this point, we do not expect a strong seasonal hiring environment in 2025," said Challenger, Gray and Christmas.

Smile mandate

While Target (TGT) is yet to announce its holiday hiring plans, the retailer is hoping a new staff policy will draw more shoppers heading into the holiday season, USA Today reported.

The policy - called the 10-4 program - requires employees within 10 feet of shoppers to smile, make eye contact and wave. If shoppers come closer, within 4 feet, employees must greet them and initiate a warm helpful interaction.

"Heading into the holiday, we're making adjustments and implementing new ways to increase connection during the most important time of the year," said Adrienne Costanzo, Target's (TGT) chief stores officer.

data.title: ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE reports Q3 results
data.description:
• ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE press release (PBSFF): Q3 adjusted net income increased by EUR 60 million to EUR 91 million in the third quarter.

  • Group revenues of EUR 820 million (previous year: EUR 882 million).

data.title: Bitfarms GAAP EPS of -$0.08 misses by $0.06, revenue of $69.25M misses by $15.41M
data.description:
• Bitfarms press release (BITF): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$0.08 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $69.25M (+155.8% Y/Y) misses by $15.41M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA* of $20 million, or 28% of revenue, up from $2 million or 8% of revenue in Q3 2024
  • Gross mining margin* of 35%, down from 44% in Q3 2024
  • The Company earned 520 BTC at an average Direct Cost per BTC* of $48,200

data.title: Quhuo expands use of blockchain technology; stock down
data.description:
• Quhuo (QH) Thursday announced a strategic partnership with blockchain investment and advisory firm Topliquidity Management.
  • Under the agreement, Topliquidity will provide advisory services to Quhuo in connection with its blockchain initiatives and digital currency strategies, strengthening its global expansion.
  • Quhuo International, the company's overseas division, focuses on vehicle exports.
  • Quhuo will actively explore compliance frameworks for blockchain technology to ensure that stablecoin payments and digital asset management operate legally and in line with regulatory requirements across different jurisdictions, the company said.
  • Quhuo (QH) stock is down about 5% in premarket activity.

data.title: Dillard's GAAP EPS of $8.31 beats by $2.14, revenue of $1.49B beats by $60M
data.description:
• Dillard's press release (DDS): Q3 GAAP EPS of $8.31 beats by $2.14.
  • Revenue of $1.49B (+2.8% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Total retail sales increased 3%
  • Comparable store sales increased 3%
  • Shares +5% PM.

data.title: Sally Beauty beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY outlook
data.description:
• Sally Beauty press release (SBH): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.55 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $947M (+1.3% Y/Y) beats by $14.12M.
  • Consolidated comparable sales growth of 1.3%.
  • Global e-commerce sales of $105 million, representing 11.1% of net sales.
  • Cash flow from operations of $121 million and Free Cash Flow of $78 million; and
  • Completed $21 million in term loan repayment and $20 million in share repurchases.
  • Fourth quarter cash flow from operations was $121 million and Free Cash Flow totaled $78 million.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance*

    Full Year    
    Consolidated Net Sales   $3.71 billion to $3.77 billion vs. consensus of $3.73B
    Comparable Sales   Flat to up 1% 
    Adjusted Operating Earnings    $328 million to $342 million
    Adjusted Diluted EPS   $2.00 to $2.10 vs. consensus of $2.01
    Capital Expenditures   Approximately $100 million
    Free Cash Flow   Approximately $200 million
    (1) Assumes approximately 50 basis points of favorable impact from expected foreign currency rates
    (2) Assumes 50% of Free Cash Flow goes towards share repurchases (Free Cash Flow defined as GAAP cash flows from operating activities less payments for capital expenditures)
    First Quarter    
    Consolidated Net Sales   $935 million to $945 million vs. consensus of $946.43M
    Comparable Sales   Approximately Flat
    Adjusted Operating Earnings    $75 million to $80 million
    Adjusted Diluted EPS   $0.43 to $0.47  vs. consensus of $0.50

data.title: Accelerant Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of $0.38 beats by $0.17, revenue of $267.4M beats by $11.94M
data.description:
• Accelerant Holdings press release (ARX): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.38 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $267.4M (+74.0% Y/Y) beats by $11.94M.
  • Exchange Written Premium of $1.043 billion grew 17% year-over-year and 39% on a year-to-date basis
  • Net loss of $1.367 billion driven by non-cash and equity-neutral profits interest distribution expenses

data.title: Brookfield GAAP EPS of $0.08, revenue of $18.92B
data.description:
• Brookfield press release (BN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.08.
  • Revenue of $18.92B (-8.2% Y/Y).
  • Total consolidated net income was $284 million for the quarter and $1.7 billion for the last twelve months (“LTM”). Distributable earnings before realizations were $1.3 billion ($0.56/share) for the quarter and $5.4 billion ($2.27/share) for the last twelve months.
  • During the quarter and LTM, earnings from realizations were $154 million and $642 million, with total DE for the quarter and the LTM of $1.5 billion ($0.63/share) and $6.0 billion ($2.54/share), respectively.
  • Asset Management, DE was $687 million ($0.29/share) in the quarter and $2.7 billion ($1.14/share) over the LTM.
  • Fee-related earnings increased by 17% from the prior year quarter to a record $754 million, supported by fee-bearing capital of $581 billion.
  • Total inflows during the quarter were $30 billion, the highest fundraising period in three years, including over $6 billion from our retail and wealth clients.

data.title: Bitfarms to convert Washington site for high-performance computing and AI workloads
data.description:
• Bitfarms (BITF) on Thursday announced its plans for converting its Washington site to HPC/AI workloads.
  • The company’s 18 MW Bitcoin mining facility in Washington State will be the first site fully converted to support HPC/AI workloads with up to 190 kW per rack and advanced liquid cooling, it said in a statement.
  • Bitfarms has signed a fully funded, binding agreement of $128 million with a large publicly traded American multinational provider of critical infrastructure and services for data centers.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, the partner will supply all critical IT equipment and building materials for 18 MW of gross capacity with an anticipated industry leading PuE, between 1.2 and 1.3. The site is targeted for completion in December 2026.
